title: rosa canina subvar. lasiostylis
intro: Rosa canina subvar. lasiostylis, also known as the Sweet Briar Rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, a deciduous shrub native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant's medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid. The health benefits of Rosa canina subvar. lasiostylis include treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
